requirements.title: ORM Selection
requirements.description: Select and document the ORM framework for the project.

requirements.title: Migration Strategy
requirements.description: Define how current schema and repository methods move to ORM without breaking CLI runs.

requirements.title: Database Access Rule
requirements.description: Establish future rule that database operations go through ORM/repository abstractions.

constraints.constraint: Future database operations MUST use an ORM/query-builder abstraction instead of hand-written repository SQL.
constraints.rationale: User explicitly required ORM-only future database operations.

constraints.constraint: Drizzle ORM SHOULD be the default recommendation for DemandRadar.
constraints.rationale: Best fit for TypeScript, SQLite, and current better-sqlite3 usage.

constraints.constraint: Raw SQL MUST be isolated behind explicit escape-hatch helpers and MUST NOT appear in feature/pipeline code.
constraints.rationale: Preserves ORM-only rule while allowing exceptional optimized SQL.

domain.problem_statement: DemandRadar needs a TypeScript ORM strategy for its SQLite storage layer.

terminology.definition: Current class that hides SQL statements from pipeline and CLI commands.
TypeScript database abstraction used for schema, queries, transactions, and migrations.

non_goals.title: No implementation in brainstorm
non_goals.rationale: The user requested research and decision guidance.

insights.summary: Drizzle should replace string SQL schema while preserving foreign keys and indexes.
ORM migration should remain storage-local behind DemandRadarRepository.
Migration requires parity tests for JSON columns, transactions, foreign keys, and read APIs.

open_questions.question: Should implementation use drizzle-kit migrations immediately or first add schema-only Drizzle queries behind the existing schema bootstrap?
